summary_one_line: Input must contain a '<![CDATA[' opener immediately followed by content that terminates after at most one valid char with an invalid/EOF byte for s.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: Bytes spelling the literal '<![CDATA[' opener (matched by CMP9 in the caller and CMP6 inside xmlParseCDSect), followed by content where the first char r is a valid XML char but the second lookahead char s is NOT a valid char (truncated/short CDATA section ending in EOF or an invalid byte right after one content character).
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: value_profile winner seeds carry a '<![CDATA[' token (e.g. Seed 2 has bytes '<![CDATA[' at offset 0x33) so the parser enters xmlParseCDSect and reaches the s = CUR_CHAR(sl) read; the section is short enough that s evaluates to an invalid char, driving the !IS_CHAR(s) TRUE branch (W marks lines 9891-9893). The CMP_MAP distance feedback over the CMP6/CMP9 magic-byte comparisons gives the winner incremental credit for getting closer to the 'CDATA[' literal, so partial matches are preserved as corpus and refined into the full opener.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: naive loser seeds either never assemble the full '<![CDATA[' opener (their early bytes are 'xlink:type', 'http:/xml', '!DOCTYPE' fragments, not the CDATA literal) or, when they do enter the section, they supply enough valid content that s is a valid char and the FALSE branch into the copy loop is taken instead (L marks lines 9894-9941 and the d=1 blocker row shows L T=0 F=8, never reaching the TRUE side). Without CMP_MAP distance feedback naive gets no gradient toward the multi-byte 'CDATA[' literal and rarely lands the precise truncated-after-one-char shape.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: value_profile's CMP_MAP records the operand-distance bucket for the CMP6('C','D','A','T','A','[') and CMP9 multi-byte comparisons gating CDATA entry; an input whose bytes are closer to matching the 'CDATA[' literal opens a new CMP_MAP bucket and is preserved, giving value_profile a Hamming/prefix-distance gradient toward assembling the opener that naive (edge-counter only) lacks.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the !IS_CHAR(s) test at parser.c:9890 inside xmlParseCDSect and its globally-blocked side is the true branch.
Reaching the blocker requires matching the CDATA opener literal via CMP6 inside the function.
The caller dispatches into xmlParseCDSect only when a 9-byte CDATA opener literal is matched.
The winner takes the true side (lines 9891-9893 marked W) while the loser continues into the copy loop (lines 9894+ marked L).
At the blocker line the winner takes the true side (T=4) and the loser only the false side (T=0 F=8).
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L9890  T=4 F=0  T=0 F=8  if (!IS_CHAR(s)) {  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: A value_profile winner seed contains the literal CDATA opener bytes.
naive loser seeds carry xlink:type fragments rather than a CDATA opener.
value_profile adds CMP_MAP buckets when an input's CMP operands are closer to matching, preserving partial-match inputs as corpus.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
An input that produces a
CMP-operand pair closer to matching than any previously-seen pair
adds a new CMP_MAP bucket and is preserved as corpus.

evidence_trail.claim: The distance bucket encodes matching-prefix length for string/memory comparisons, providing a gradient toward multi-byte literals.
value_profile won 9/10 trials versus naive's 1/10 at this branch, a significant divergence.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: Lineage verification is not mandatory for CMP_MAP gradient (only for I2SRandReplace). The mechanism is supported directly by the prompt: value_profile's CMP_MAP distance feedback preserves inputs whose operands approach the 'CDATA[' literal, and the winner seeds carry the assembled '<![CDATA[' opener bytes while naive losers do not. The MECHANISM CONTEXT explicitly notes value_profile has no I2SRandReplace, so substitution is not the driver.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness gating a goto-out true branch behind a multi-byte literal opener in which naive reaches the truncated-after-one-char true side as often as value_profile (no value_profile advantage), showing the CMP_MAP distance gradient does not drive opener assembly.

weakest_evidence_point: Whether value_profile's advantage is dominated by assembling the multi-byte CDATA opener literal versus the precise truncated-after-one-char content shape that makes s invalid is not fully separable from the available seed/byte evidence.
